expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` (or equivalent inspection step) before any build/benchmark work.
expectations.evidence: Baseline doesn't use winml inspect; goes straight to optimum-cli export.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite NPU.
expectations.evidence: General knowledge identifies QNN / Hexagon NPU.

expectations.text: Recommends building the model once and then running `winml perf` twice -- once on NPU, once on CPU -- rather than building two separate pipelines.
expectations.evidence: Baseline exports once but quantizes separately for NPU (separate QDQ file), then benchmarks. Doesn't have the 'shared build, two perf runs' shape.

expectations.text: Specifies that the CPU run should use the optimized (pre-compile) artifact, NOT the QNN-compiled artifact.
expectations.evidence: Baseline has no notion of an EP-compiled artifact -- uses FP32 ONNX for CPU and a separately quantized QDQ ONNX for NPU.

expectations.text: Warns that EP-compiled artifacts are EP-locked / can't be run on a different EP and produce nonsense numbers.
Either directs user to `winml --help`, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
Includes an install/prereq block (or a clear pointer back to install steps) -- the user's prompt does not signal prior winml usage, so the default-include-install rule applies.
expectations.evidence: Baseline installs onnxruntime-qnn / optimum, not the winml CLI.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: Response uses no winml commands (trivially passes).
